evidence: .github/actions/GithubToken/generate_token.py executes env reads, JWT generation, network I/O (`requests.post`), and file writes at module import time (lines 10-56), making import side effects unavoidable.

examples/a2a_bridge/02_with_auth.py and examples/a2a_gateway/01_basic_gateway.py call `setup_logging()` at module import time (lines 30 and 59), so importing these scripts mutates global logging configuration outside explicit entrypoint control.

tests/integration/conftest.py is a re-export hub importing many fixtures/markers from another module (lines 9-34), while tests/integration/test_smoke.py imports from that conftest module (line 8), coupling test modules to pytest wiring internals.

tests/runtime/test_presence.py, tests/runtime/test_runtime.py, and tests/integration/test_smoke.py import helpers/markers from shared conftest modules (tests.conftest and tests.integration.conftest), concentrating cross-test dependencies into central hubs.
examples/coding_agents/test_communication.py mutates `sys.path` (line 24) and relies on ad-hoc runtime import topology, bypassing package boundaries and creating path-order dependency.

Error-handling conventions diverge across sibling integrations: `src/thenvoi/integrations/langgraph/langchain_tools.py` catches broad exceptions in nearly every wrapper and returns error strings, while `examples/a2a_gateway/demo_orchestrator/remote_agent.py` and `docker/shared/config_loader.py` convert failures into raised exceptions (`RuntimeError`/`ValueError`).

Package export conventions are inconsistent at the top-level API boundary: `src/thenvoi/__init__.py` and `src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` expose very large re-export surfaces, while sibling integration packages like `src/thenvoi/integrations/anthropic/__init__.py`, `src/thenvoi/integrations/parlant/__init__.py`, and `src/thenvoi/integrations/pydantic_ai/__init__.py` export empty `__all__`.

Example logging modules implement divergent setup contracts for the same role (`setup_logging`): `examples/codex/setup_logging.py` customizes root logger, stream target, and format, while most sibling modules only pass a level and optional extra logger names.

Near-duplicate tool package initializers use different registration idioms without a shared convention (`examples/claude_sdk_docker/tools/__init__.py` uses tuple-to-dict staging while `examples/coding_agents/tools/__init__.py` uses direct literal registry).

findings.summary: Entrypoint modules perform heavy side effects at import time, creating order-dependent coupling and unsafe reuse as libraries.
evidence: .github/actions/GithubToken/generate_token.py executes external HTTP (`requests.post`) and writes `GITHUB_OUTPUT` during module import (lines 39-54), with no `main()` guard.
examples/a2a_bridge/02_with_auth.py and examples/a2a_gateway/01_basic_gateway.py call `setup_logging()` at top-level (lines 30 and 59), applying process-global config on import.
findings.suggestion: Move all side effects behind explicit `main()`/`run()` entrypoints, keep top-level code declaration-only, and gate execution with `if __name__ == "__main__":` for script paths; expose callable functions for reuse and testing.

findings.summary: Tests depend directly on conftest modules as shared APIs, turning pytest wiring files into cross-module dependency hubs.
evidence: tests/integration/test_smoke.py imports `requires_api` from `tests.integration.conftest` (line 8), coupling test logic to pytest config module internals.

tests/runtime/test_presence.py and tests/runtime/test_runtime.py import helper constructors from `tests.conftest` (lines 10-14 and line 11), using conftest as a utility library rather than fixture registration boundary.
tests/integration/conftest.py re-exports a broad symbol set (lines 9-34 and `__all__` lines 36-61), reinforcing a central hub pattern with large blast radius.
findings.suggestion: Extract reusable test helpers/markers into dedicated utility modules (e.g., `tests/helpers/events.py`, `tests/helpers/markers.py`) and keep `conftest.py` focused on fixture/plugin registration only.

findings.summary: Example test script bypasses package boundaries via direct `sys.path` mutation, making module resolution environment-dependent.
evidence: examples/coding_agents/test_communication.py inserts a relative repo path into `sys.path` (line 24), creating implicit import coupling to local filesystem layout.
Other examples rely on module imports without path mutation, causing inconsistent cross-module bootstrapping behavior across scripts.
findings.suggestion: Package example dependencies properly and import via stable package paths; if needed, run examples through `uv run -m ...` entrypoints rather than mutating `sys.path` in code.

findings.summary: Codex stdio and websocket clients duplicate lifecycle/read-loop responsibilities, increasing boundary drift risk between transports.
evidence: Both transport clients independently implement connect/close/read-loop task orchestration and pending-failure semantics (`_connected`, `_reader_task`, `_fail_pending`) with similar but separate logic.
Stdio client adds stderr loop/process termination behavior while websocket client has its own close/disconnect path, making transport lifecycle guarantees distributed instead of centrally enforced.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared transport lifecycle abstraction in the integration layer (task management, state transitions, pending-failure contract), and keep each client responsible only for transport-specific send/receive primitives.

findings.summary: Tool-layer failures are surfaced with incompatible contracts (string-returned errors vs raised exceptions) across adjacent integrations.
evidence: `agent_tools_to_langchain` wrappers catch `Exception` and return strings like `"Error ..."` for send/add/remove/contact/memory operations.
`create_parlant_tools` similarly catches exceptions and returns `ToolResult(data="Error ...")` instead of propagating typed failures.
`GatewayClient.call_peer` catches and re-raises as `RuntimeError`, and `load_config` raises `ValueError` for file/YAML errors, creating a different failure protocol for callers.
findings.suggestion: Define a single cross-integration tool error convention (for example: structured error object with code/message/details, or always raise typed domain exceptions at adapter boundaries) and refactor LangGraph/Parlant/A2A helper layers to follow it consistently.

findings.summary: Public package `__init__` files follow conflicting export philosophies, making API discovery inconsistent.
evidence: `src/thenvoi/__init__.py` re-exports a broad mixed surface (composition/platform/runtime/types/tools/formatters/trackers/shutdown helpers).
`src/thenvoi/runtime/__init__.py` repeats another broad aggregator pattern, while multiple sibling integration packages expose empty `__all__` and no symbols.
The result is that similar package levels do not follow a predictable import contract (facade-heavy vs intentionally empty).
findings.suggestion: Standardize `__init__` policy by package tier (e.g., thin facades with curated stable symbols, no deep internal re-exports, and explicit rationale for empty facades) and apply it consistently across core/runtime/integrations.

findings.summary: Sibling example modules expose the same `setup_logging` entry point but with materially different behavior and options.
evidence: `examples/codex/setup_logging.py` configures root level, stdout streaming, custom format/date handling, and warning logger suppression.
`examples/a2a_gateway/setup_logging.py` sets `root_level` and warning logger overrides, while `examples/langgraph/setup_logging.py` passes only level with default behavior.
`examples/a2a_bridge/setup_logging.py` adds an extra boolean (`a2a_debug`) absent in other siblings, creating multiple incompatible invocation conventions.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared example logging profile abstraction (named presets plus consistent function signature) and have each example module call that profile instead of ad-hoc per-directory contracts.

findings.summary: Two example tool packages implement nearly identical registries with different structure, creating avoidable style islands.
evidence: Both packages re-export the same tool set (`calculator`, `get_time`, `random_number`) and both example_tools modules are identical namespace re-exports from `thenvoi.testing.example_tools`.
Registry assembly differs (`_TOOLS` tuple then `dict(_TOOLS)` vs direct dict literal), without any behavioral reason indicated in code comments/docs.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single shared registry builder (or one shared tools package) and have both example directories import it to remove convention drift and reduce duplicate maintenance surfaces.

findings.summary: README documents an optional dependency key that does not exist in package metadata, breaking the install contract across modules.
evidence: README installation commands include `git+https://github.com/thenvoi/thenvoi-sdk-python.git[pydantic_ai]`.
`pyproject.toml` defines the extra as `pydantic-ai` (hyphen), not `pydantic_ai` (underscore).
This mismatch makes docs-driven installs fail or select the wrong dependency target.
findings.suggestion: Normalize extra naming to one canonical form and enforce it with a CI docs-check (e.g., script validating README extras against `[project.optional-dependencies]` keys) so package and documentation contracts cannot drift.

findings.summary: README’s package topology references modules that differ from runtime paths, reducing trust in documented boundaries.
evidence: README package structure lists `platform/events.py`.
Tests import event types from `thenvoi.platform.event` (singular), matching runtime module `src/thenvoi/platform/event.py`.
The public architecture map is therefore inconsistent with actual import boundaries used by code.
findings.suggestion: Generate or validate the package-structure section from real filesystem/import paths in CI so docs reflect actual module boundaries and avoid stale architecture contracts.

findings.summary: Cross-module surfaces presented as production workflows are excluded from type-check quality gates.
evidence: README marks multiple integrations as production-ready and documents A2A bridge/gateway workflows.
`pyproject.toml` pyrefly config excludes `examples/**` and `thenvoi-bridge/**` entirely.
Assigned bridge/orchestrator modules therefore sit outside static typing gates despite being key cross-module contract demonstrations.
findings.suggestion: Split demo-only scripts from contract-bearing integration modules, then include contract-bearing paths (at least `thenvoi-bridge/bridge_core/**` and orchestrator library modules) in a separate strict type-check job.

findings.summary: The orchestrator tool layer is bound to a process-global gateway client, creating hidden cross-instance coupling and boundary leakage.
evidence: `agent.py` defines module-global `_gateway_client` and mutates it in `set_gateway_client()` during each `OrchestratorAgent` initialization.
`call_peer_agent` uses that global instead of instance state, so multiple orchestrators in one process share/overwrite transport target implicitly.
`remote_agent.py` models `GatewayClient` as an instance object, but the tool binding bypasses instance encapsulation.
findings.suggestion: Eliminate module-global tool state by binding tool functions per agent instance (closure or class-bound tool factory) so each orchestrator owns its own `GatewayClient` and concurrency isolation is explicit.

findings.summary: Bridge session eviction depends on an external healthcheck loop, but that lifecycle dependency is only implicit in code comments.
evidence: `InMemorySessionStore` performs TTL eviction only on `list_sessions()`/`count()`, not on `get()`/`get_or_create()` hot paths.
Docstring states production behavior relies on Docker healthcheck polling `/health` every 30s to trigger eviction.
README does not define this as a required runtime contract for bridge deployments, so module boundary responsibilities are implicit rather than enforced.
findings.suggestion: Make eviction lifecycle explicit in architecture contracts by either adding an internal background eviction task in bridge runtime or documenting/enforcing healthcheck requirements in bridge setup docs and deployment checks.
